title: senecio eriopus subsp. hosmariensis
intro: Senecio eriopus subsp. hosmariensis, a subspecies of the Jacob's Ladder plant, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Channel Islands in the English Channel. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Senecio eriopus subsp. hosmariensis include its ability to reduce coughs and relieve congestion, as well as its potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to use it under the guidance of a qualified practitioner due to its potential toxicity.
